what_it_does: This file is a metadata-only channel attachment record that associates an attachment identifier with a channel, an attachment type, and a target board item URI.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an id 'att-e00d3954' and channel 'dragnet-development'.
The attachment_type is 'board_items' and the target is 'board://item-d44aea08'.
No code symbols are defined in the file.
standouts: Typed link to a board item via the 'board://item-d44aea08' URI.
Attachment type 'board_items' suggests a cross-tool reference.
Precise ISO timestamp for when the attachment was recorded.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable id and UTC timestamp.
Uses a 'board://' URI scheme to reference an external board item.
Keeps the attachment record minimal and reference-only.
concerns: The file contains no description of what the referenced board item is.
There is no author or provenance information for the attachment record.
notes: A bare channel-to-board attachment record that links dragnet-development memory to board item item-d44aea08, created on 2026-06-24.
questions: What content or topic does board://item-d44aea08 represent?
What is the intended semantic of the 'board_items' attachment type?
Who or what process created this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — the file records an attachment reference but does not state an explicit purpose beyond its fields.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, typed association between a channel memory artifact and a specific board item so the two can be retrieved together.
novelty: The use of a 'board://' URI scheme as the target of an otherwise generic attachment metadata file.
contribution: It contributes a persistent, typed link between a channel-memory attachment and a board item, along with a creation timestamp, enabling cross-context lookup.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record linking dragnet-development attachment att-e00d3954 to board item board://item-d44aea08 with a 2026-06-24 timestamp.
